The family Polypodiaceae is a large and diverse group of ferns, with over 2,000 species worldwide. Lacostea brachypus is classified as a genus within this family, with its specific classification being Lacostea brachypus, belonging to the kingdom Plantae. As a member of the family Polypodiaceae, Lacostea brachypus is further classified into the division Pteridophyta, a group of vascular plants that reproduce via spores. The classification of Lacostea brachypus is subject to ongoing research and revision in the field of botany.

intro: Lacostea brachypus, also known as Lacostea, is a plant species that belongs to the family Araliaceae and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, which can help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, wounds, and infections.
